title: fix(#1802): code review findings + reviews land in BigQuery
rationale: linked to issue #1802, recent bot review with deferred MINOR (publishFinding re-fetches PR) — tests whether enriched skill defers correctly
body:
Fixes #1802. Follow-up to #1789 / #1790.

## Summary

After #1790, `code_review_pull_requests` populates and the dashboard PR
funnel renders, but every `finding_recorded` and `review_completed` event
still dead-letters at BigQuery insertion. Live prod DLQ when this PR was
opened:

```
finding_recorded → JSON is missing required field: sha, bot, rule_slug, ingestion_source
review_completed → JSON is missing required field: repository, pr, sha, bot,
                   model, model_full_path, skill_version, review_attempt, ingestion_source
```

This PR rewrites both publisher payloads to match the
`aictrl_telemetry.code_review_findings` and
`aictrl_telemetry.code_review_runs` table schemas so BQ inserts succeed
and the dashboard's Findings Volume, Actionability, Review Duration, and
Top Noisy Rules cards populate.

## Changes

`server/services/code-review-publisher.ts`
- `publishFinding`: rename `start_line`→`line`, `suggestion`→`suggested_fix`;
  drop `title` (mapped into `summary` when ≤60 chars per BQ column limit);
  drop top-level `pr_id` (not in BQ schema). Add REQUIRED columns: `sha`
  (denormalised from `PR.currentSha`), `bot='aictrl-dev'`, `rule_slug='other'`,
  `ingestion_source='mcp'`. Add NULLABLE `repository` + `pr` structs
  (denormalised from parent PR via `getPullRequestManager()`). Missing
  parent PR is non-fatal — emits null structs + `sha='unknown'`.
- `publishReviewCompleted`: emit all REQUIRED columns: `repository`, `pr`,
  `sha`, `bot`, `model` (struct + denormalised `model_full_path`),
  `skill_version` (from `review.skillId ?? 'unknown'`), `review_attempt=1`,
  `ingestion_source='mcp'`. `token_usage` now uses snake_case nested
  fields (`cache_read`, `cache_write`). Missing parent PR is FATAL — bails
  loudly because `repository`/`pr` are REQUIRED on the runs table.
- Two helpers — `buildRepositoryStruct` and `buildPrStruct` — shared by
  both publishers; match the BQ struct shapes exactly.

## Lossy defaults (intentional, tracked as follow-ups on #1802)

These satisfy BQ REQUIRED constraints today so the dashboard can light up
immediately. Each is a follow-up to plumb real values through:

- `model = { provider: 'unknown', name: 'unknown', full_path: 'unknown' }`
  — `record_review_started` MCP arg doesn't yet capture the model identity.
- `rule_slug = 'other'` — `record_finding` MCP arg doesn't yet capture the
  finding category. All findings will land under "other" in noisy-rules
  until plumbed.
- `review_attempt = 1` — only one attempt per (PR, SHA) in the current
  flow; if we re-review the same SHA this would need to derive from prior
  review docs.

## Test plan

- [x] `npx vitest run test/code-review-publisher.test.ts` — 22 tests pass
      (was 12 before this PR; added 10 BQ-schema-coverage tests for both
      publishers including parent-PR-missing edge cases and the
      summary-truncation rule)
- [x] `npx vitest run test/code-review-publisher.test.ts test/mcp/code-review.test.ts test/api/github-app-routes.test.ts test/code-review/` — 164 tests pass (no regressions on PR pipeline)
- [x] `npm run lint` — clean
- [x] `npx ast-grep scan` — only the pre-existing warning in `step-matcher.ts:24` (unrelated)
- [x] `npm run check:types-mirror` — 10 types OK
- [x] `npm run check:knip` — clean
- [x] `npm run build` — TypeScript compile passes
- [ ] After deploy to sandbox: `aictrl_telemetry.code_review_findings` and `code_review_runs` populate within 1 review cycle; DLQ stops accumulating new finding/review messages
- [ ] Dashboard cards (Findings Volume, Actionability, Review Duration, Top Noisy Rules) render with data on sandbox

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

botReviews.body:
## Code Review — aictrl-dev

**PR:** #1803 — fix(#1802): code review findings + reviews land in BigQuery
**Files reviewed:** 2 (+363/−36)
**Findings:** 6 (1 MAJOR · 1 MINOR · 4 NIT)

---

### MAJOR

| # | File | Line | Summary |
|---|------|------|---------|
| 1 | `server/services/code-review-publisher.ts` | 386 | Transient PR fetch error permanently drops review event |

**#1 — Transient PR fetch error permanently drops review event**
`publishReviewCompleted` catches ALL errors from `getPullRequest` (including transient network/timeout errors) and returns without publishing. Since the function returns void and never throws, the caller treats this as success. A temporary Firestore blip permanently loses the `review_completed` event with no retry, dead-letter, or recovery path.

```typescript
  try {
    pr = await getPullRequestManager().getPullRequest(review.orgId, review.prId);
  } catch (err) {
    log.error(
      { err, reviewId: review.id, prId: review.prId, orgId: review.orgId },
      '[CodeReviewPublisher] Could not load parent PR for review completion — skipping publish',
    );
    return;
  }
```

**Suggestion:** Only bail on "not found" (null result). For caught exceptions, consider still publishing with `repository`/`pr` as null (like `publishFinding` does) or re-throwing so the caller's `.catch()` path can log it as a publish failure.

---

### MINOR

| # | File | Line | Summary |
|---|------|------|---------|
| 2 | `server/services/code-review-publisher.ts` | 294 | Each finding fetches the parent PR individually |

**#2 — Each finding fetches the parent PR individually**
`publishFinding` calls `getPullRequestManager().getPullRequest()` on every invocation. A review with 50 findings results in 50 identical PR reads across 50 separate `record_finding` MCP calls. The PR data is immutable during a review, so it could be fetched once by the caller and passed into `publishFinding`, or cached at the manager level.

**Suggestion:** Add an optional `pr?: PullRequest` parameter to `publishFinding` so callers that already have the PR can pass it through, avoiding the redundant fetch. Fall back to the current `getPullRequest` path when omitted.

---

### NIT

| # | File | Line | Summary |
|---|------|------|---------|
| 3 | `server/services/code-review-publisher.ts` | 299 | Inline magic number 60 for BQ summary limit |
| 4 | `server/services/code-review-publisher.ts` | 248 | Inconsistent `?? null` in `buildRepositoryStruct` |
| 5 | `server/services/code-review-publisher.ts` | 319 | `sha='unknown'` writes invalid placeholder to BQ |
| 6 | `test/code-review-publisher.test.ts` | 469 | Deprecated field rename `execution_id` not tested |

**#3 — Inline magic number 60 for BQ summary limit**
Every other BQ-related default (`DEFAULT_BOT`, `DEFAULT_MODEL`, `DEFAULT_RULE_SLUG`) is a named constant, but the 60-char truncation limit for `summary` is an inline magic number. A named constant would stay in sync with the BQ schema definition.
```typescript
summary: finding.title.length <= 60 ? finding.title : null,
```
**Suggestion:** Extract `const BQ_SUMMARY_MAX_LENGTH = 60;` near the other `DEFAULT_*` constants.

**#4 — Inconsistent `?? null` in `buildRepositoryStruct`**
`buildRepositoryStruct` applies `?? null` to `id`, `url`, and `default_branch` but omits it on `full_name` and `platform`. A future refactor making those fields optional would silently emit `undefined` instead of `null` to BQ.

**#5 — `sha='unknown'` writes invalid placeholder to BQ**
When the parent PR can't be loaded, `sha` falls back to the literal string `'unknown'` (7 chars) instead of a 40-char hex SHA. This creates noise in BQ queries and dashboards that filter or group by `sha`. Consider using `null` (if the column is nullable) to make the missing-data case distinguishable.

**#6 — Deprecated field rename `execution_id` not tested**
The "drops deprecated fields" test asserts absence of `pr_id`, `status`, `findings_by_severity`, and `skill_id`, but the code also renames `execution_id` to `execution_run_id`. The old name `execution_id` should be asserted absent for parity.
```typescript
expect(json).not.toHaveProperty('pr_id');
expect(json).not.toHaveProperty('status');
expect(json).not.toHaveProperty('findings_by_severity');
expect(json).not.toHaveProperty('skill_id'); // renamed → skill_version
```
**Suggestion:** Add `expect(json).not.toHaveProperty('execution_id');`.

---

Reviewed SHA: 2bf17b428043c72ac26a3f19f7bfc350552b18e4

findings.description: Transient PR fetch error permanently drops review_completed event — caught errors silently early-return without throwing
findings.reason: re-throw on caught errors so the caller's .catch() surfaces the failure for retry; null-PR early return preserved as the genuine data-integrity case

findings.description: publishFinding re-fetches the parent PR on every call — N findings = N identical Firestore reads
findings.reason: cross-cutting refactor; same codepath touched by #1797 — better to do both denormalisation changes together

findings.description: Inline magic number 60 for BQ summary truncation — every other BQ default is a named constant
findings.reason: extracted BQ_SUMMARY_MAX_LENGTH near the other DEFAULT_* constants

findings.description: buildRepositoryStruct inconsistent ?? null — full_name and platform omitted
findings.reason: added ?? null to all five fields for parity; comment explains defence against schema drift

findings.description: sha='unknown' writes invalid placeholder to BQ — 7 chars vs 40-char hex
findings.reason: extracted UNKNOWN_SHA constant; can't switch to null because sha is REQUIRED on both BQ tables — comment documents the trade-off

findings.description: execution_id rename not asserted — old name should appear in the deprecated-fields test parity with skill_id
findings.reason: added missing assertion at end of the deprecated-fields test
